title: entada rheedei subsp. rheedei
intro: Entada rheedei subsp. rheedei, also known as the Rheede's entada, is a climbing plant native to the tropical forests of India and Southeast Asia. It is a type of legume that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as lowering blood pressure, reducing fever, and alleviating digestive issues, making it a valuable resource for herbal remedies. The herbal preparations of Entada rheedei subsp. rheedei include extracts, decoctions, and infusions that can be used to treat various ailments, with the root and bark being the most commonly used parts for medicinal purposes.
